subject: Chemistry
title: Humphry Davy's Electric Elements
description: A quick win: understand Humphry Davy's Electric Elements.

hook.text: Humphry Davy used a giant battery to rip apart compounds nobody had ever broken before—and found new elements.

buildup.text: In 1807, he passed electric current through molten potash. A shiny metal appeared on the electrode.

discovery.text: He had isolated potassium. Within weeks, he did the same with sodium—two elements never seen before.

twist.text: Davy discovered potassium, sodium, calcium, barium, magnesium, and boron—all with electricity.

climax.text: He proved electricity could unlock nature's building blocks. Electrochemistry was born.

punchline.text: One battery, six new elements. Davy rewrote the table.

quiz.question: How did Humphry Davy discover new elements?
options: By passing electricity through molten compounds
By heating rocks to extreme temperatures
